title: Block 2 mid-point: paragraph practice
description: Real paragraph with full punctuation set — preview of writing fluency
text: we work hard, study every day, and never give up. "do we?" she asked. "yes!" they said. it's a good life, and we share it with friends.

tips.text: This is the longest passage you have met, and it carries the full kit of punctuation. Read it as a paragraph with a voice in it, not a string of keys.
If a comma or a quotation mark slows you down, that is no fault. The marks come fast enough once you stop reaching for them and start placing them.

tips.target: we work hard, study every day, and never give up. "do we?" she

tips.text: You are a fifth of the way through the block. Numbers and symbols wait further on; for now, hold this paragraph clean to the end.

tips.target: asked. "yes!" they said. it's a good life, and we share it with friends.

character_tips.anna: The middle of the block, and you are already setting real paragraphs with proper marks. Numbers and symbols are the road ahead. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: A long passage with every mark in its place is the work a desk does all day. You are doing it. Keep on. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Halfway through the block, with the punctuation behaving. Numbers come next; hold your pace until then. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Halfway, and the marks no longer trip you. That is steady progress, the kind that lasts. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
